name: Peaches Poached in Red Wine with Pepper
recipeIngredient: 1/2 bottle red wine (Cote du Rhone or Pinot Noir)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the peppercorns in a square of cheesecloth and secure with string.
In a large saucepan, combine the port, lemon peel, vanilla bean, bay leaves,
wine, and pepper sachet. Bring to a boil. Stir in the honey and remove the
saucepan from heat. Add more honey if necessary. Cover the saucepan and set aside.
2. Half fill a large saucepan with water and bring to a boil. Plunge peaches into boiling water for 1-2 minutes,
then rinse under cold water and peel them. Place in warm wine over medium heat and simmer until just tender, about
10 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and let cool.
3. Transfer fruit to a shallow bowl. Remove bay leaves and place one at the stem where the leaves would be.
Cut vanilla bean into six segments and place one segment in each peach at the stem. Set aside in a cool place,
away from the refrigerator, until ready to serve.
